title: Ovarian Tissue Procurement from Organ Donor
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"d5000","text":"The purpose of this protocol is to describe the procurement of human ovarian tissue from organ donors for research purposes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"amvpa","text":"Researchers will adhere to all safety and training protocols\nrequired including but not limited to: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2h648","text":"1.Biosafety Certification ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"45ff6","text":"2.Bloodborne Pathogens Certification \n3.Collaborative Institutional Training Initiative (CITI program) certification ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"al9m8","text":"Researchers will wear personal protective equipment when working with human specimens, including gloves, masks, and lab coats. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: The NU-RTL is an established IRB-approved central repository that allows the collection of clinical specimens from participants including but not limited to gynecologic tissue, follicular fluid, and associated bio-fluids. This material is obtained at the time of surgery and/or clinical procedure and would otherwise be discarded.

Reproductive specimens are placed in saline after tissue resection. The amount of tissue, the sites from which it is obtained, and the amount dedicated to the research study are at the discretion of the Attending Physician and the Department of Pathology.
The samples are transported fresh and on ice (if necessary) to the Reproductive Tissue Library Core for further processing by trained research staff.
Ovaries are evaluated grossly per standard protocol in Northwestern Medicine Pathology. Ovaries are measured, external surface is examined and evaluated for neoplasia.

The ovaries are sectioned in 3-5 mm sections perpendicular to the long axis to evaluate the cut surface (Figure 1). Figure 1: Whole ovary tissue processing by pathology before ovarian tissue is allocated for researchers.
If no significant gross pathology is found, any ovary sections that are not required for clinical diagnosis are handed to the research coordinator.

If gross lesions are found, any non-lesioned tissue based on individual assessment is provided. The sign-out pathologist or study pathologist is consulted in case of questions on what is appropriate to submit or retain.
creator: Pooja Devrukhkar
